title: Building a Second Brain for Your Agent
description: Pitch a coworker on Obsidian + Claude Code as a personal knowledge base. Casual register.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You keep mentioning Obsidian. Why not just Notion or Google Docs?

templates.text: Because Obsidian files are local Markdown. Any AI agent can read them directly.
templates.hint_zh: 因為 Obsidian 是本地 Markdown 檔。任何 AI agent 都能直接讀。

templates.text: Only through their API, with their limits. Plus your data lives on their servers.
templates.hint_zh: 只能透過他們的 API,還有限制。資料也存在他們伺服器上。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What does the AI actually do with the notes?

templates.text: Reads, links, tags, summarizes. The boring filing work I never want to do.
templates.hint_zh: 讀、連結、加標籤、做摘要。我懶得做的歸檔雜務。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What if I switch from Claude to a different agent next year?

templates.text: That's the whole point. The notes don't move. Only the agent changes.
templates.hint_zh: 重點就在這。筆記不動,只換 agent。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, sold. Where do I start?

templates.text: Make a vault, add a CLAUDE.md telling the agent what each folder is for. Then just take notes.
templates.hint_zh: 開個 vault,放一個 CLAUDE.md 告訴 agent 每個資料夾的用途。然後就開始記。
